name: Viduidae
description: Indigobirds and whydahs are a family of passerine birds endemic to sub-Saharan Africa. They are brood parasites, laying their eggs in the nests of other passerines, and are known for the elaborate tail feathers of breeding males.

children.name: Vidua
children.description: A genus of brood-parasitic finch-like birds with striking sexual dimorphism and long tail feathers in breeding males.

children.name: Vidua macroura
children.description: The Pin-tailed Whydah is a small bird with a black-and-white breeding male that has four long, pin-like tail feathers. It is a brood parasite of waxbills and is found across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Vidua paradisaea
children.description: The Long-tailed Paradise Whydah has a spectacular breeding male with a black back, yellow nape, and extremely elongated tail feathers. It parasitizes green-winged pytilias in East African savannas.

children.name: Vidua chalybeata
children.description: The Village Indigobird is a small, glossy blue-black finch-like bird with a red bill. It is a brood parasite specializing on firefinches and is common across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Vidua obtusa
children.description: The Broad-tailed Paradise Whydah has a breeding male with a broad, spatulate tail and golden-yellow nape. It parasitizes melba finches in woodlands of central and southern Africa.
